formula_note: Axis component anchored on 147,000 Sixth Army dead (minimum) and expanded modestly for other directly assigned Axis formations; Soviet component modeled conservatively from the defensive-operation casualty total and the offensive-phase killed/captured table, keeping captured personnel from being treated mechanically as deaths.
uncertainty_note: Largest uncertainty in the whole bundle. Soviet military deaths are not available as a clean city-matched direct-death series; the range brackets conservative to less-conservative conversions of mixed casualty categories and phase allocation.
quality_note: Model-based estimate built from strong casualty anchors but incomplete death-specific series.

inflicting_side: German forces (principally Luftwaffe during opening bombardment)
formula_note: Bounded by a 40,000 lower anchor and a contested 70,000 upper anchor from the opening bombardment; best estimate centered at 50,000 because much of the direct civilian killing appears to have occurred in the first week, while residual-survivor evidence implies the direct-death total did not automatically rise into far larger all-cause demographic-loss claims.
uncertainty_note: Direct civilian deaths are better anchored than military deaths, but the high end remains contested and later street-fighting deaths are not captured in a separate named-victim registry.
quality_note: Reasonably triangulated for a wartime urban battle, but still lacks a dedicated city mortality registry.

formula_note: No separate unknown-status direct-death series was located for this episode.
uncertainty_note: Strict exclusion is used in the core DR path. This does not imply true unknown-status deaths were zero; it means no defensible separate series was found.
quality_note: Unknown-status cases are omitted rather than silently folded into civilians or military.

formula_note: Piecewise combined Soviet+Axis exposure. Phase A (23 Aug-18 Nov, about 2.9 months) uses average combined strength of roughly 0.82m/0.95m/1.15m. Phase B (19 Nov-2 Feb, about 2.5 months) uses roughly 1.49m/1.62m/1.74m, anchored on Soviet 1.1435m and Axis 330k-600k region/pocket figures.
uncertainty_note: Military geometry is broader than the civilian footprint because operationally integral encirclement and relief forces were directly assigned to the urban battle. A narrower military polygon would reduce person-months and raise DR.
quality_note: Reasonable operational approximation, but not directly observed month-by-month deployment data.

formula_note: Piecewise exposure model: roughly 600k still present on 23 Aug; about 300k evacuated by 10 Sep; more than 50k remained trapped on the west bank; more than 10k were still alive in the ruins late in the battle. Scenario averages are applied across three periods to generate low/best/high person-months.
uncertainty_note: Most sensitive denominator in the bundle. Results change materially depending on how narrowly one clips the exposed civilian footprint after the main evacuation and how quickly one assumes the trapped population declined.
quality_note: Model-generated because no month-by-month city population series was found.

formula_note: (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) / (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months), using strict exclusion of unknown-status deaths.
uncertainty_note: The interval crosses 1.0, so the episode cannot be described confidently as more lethal per person-month for either civilians or military. Direction depends mainly on the modeled Soviet military-death numerator and the late-1942 civilian exposure denominator.
quality_note: Useful as a bounded scenario estimate, not as a precision statistic.
